message:
test: add SCC + path-generation fuzz target

fuzz_scc_paths drives the strongly-connected-components, biconnected,
and path-generation Rust algorithms covered by the recent conformance
harnesses (d1fedae, ffbee5e, 38faec8) so structural panics or
invariant breaks on adversarial graphs surface in continuous fuzzing.

Functions exercised:

- SCC family: strongly_connected_components (Tarjan),
  kosaraju_strongly_connected_components, condensation,
  attracting_components.
- Biconnected family: biconnected_components,
  biconnected_component_edges, articulation_points, bridges.
- Path family: all_simple_paths, all_simple_paths_directed,
  shortest_simple_paths.

Runtime invariants asserted (any break triggers a fuzz crash):

- Tarjan and Kosaraju enumerate the same set of SCCs (yield order
  may differ; compare as sorted-vector sets).
- ``condensation(G).node_count() == |strongly_connected_components(G)|``.
- Every attracting component is itself an SCC (subset relation).
- Every bridge edge belongs to a singleton biconnected component
  containing exactly that edge.
- ``shortest_simple_paths`` yields paths in non-decreasing length
  order (Yen's contract).

Smoke-tested with 1000 runs (1950 cov features, no crashes, no
invariant breaks). Seeded with 9 hand-crafted variant-tagged inputs
plus 8 representative discoveries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
